content_en:
Al-Baqillani made a lasting contribution to Islamic scholarship through his separate treatise I'jaz al-Quran and through the treatment of the same subject in At-Tamhid. His analysis of Quranic inimitability (i'jaz) became one of the most sophisticated and widely cited treatments of the subject in classical Islamic literature.

The doctrine of Quranic inimitability holds that the Quran is a miracle that demonstrates Muhammad's prophethood: it is beyond the capacity of any human being or group of human beings to produce anything comparable. The Quran itself issues a challenge (tahaddi) to those who doubt its divine origin: produce a comparable chapter. That this challenge was not met — despite the extraordinary literary culture of seventh-century Arabia and the strong motivation of the Prophet's opponents to discredit his message — is taken as evidence of the Quran's superhuman origin.

Al-Baqillani's contribution was to analyze precisely what makes the Quran inimitable. Earlier discussions had sometimes been vague on this point. He identified several distinct dimensions. Linguistically, the Quran achieves a level of Arabic eloquence and stylistic perfection — in its word choice, sentence structure, rhythm, and overall coherence — that no human author has matched. This is not merely a matter of aesthetic preference but can be analyzed through the categories of Arabic rhetoric (balagha), showing specifically why the Quran's language is superior.

Beyond linguistic excellence, al-Baqillani points to the Quran's content: its accounts of past events that Muhammad had no access to through normal historical channels, its accurate predictions of future events, its comprehensive and coherent ethical and legal teaching, and its psychological and spiritual impact on those who receive it. These dimensions of inimitability extend beyond the purely literary.

Al-Baqillani is also careful to define what the comparison involves. He analyzes pre-Islamic Arabic poetry — the mu'allaqat and other celebrated texts — and shows specifically how they differ from the Quran. He is not dismissive of Arabic literary achievement but uses it to highlight the distinctive and unmatched qualities of the Quran. This analytical approach made his treatment of i'jaz a model for subsequent scholars and a standard reference in the field of Quranic sciences.

The doctrine of i'jaz remains significant in contemporary Islamic discourse, particularly in engagements with secular or critical approaches to the Quran that treat it as a human literary document. Al-Baqillani's analytical framework, while developed in a classical context, provides tools for contemporary articulation of the traditional claim.
content_id:
Al-Baqillani memberikan kontribusi yang bertahan lama bagi keilmuan Islam melalui risalahnya yang terpisah I'jaz al-Quran dan melalui pembahasan subyek yang sama dalam At-Tamhid. Analisisnya tentang kemukjizatan Al-Quran (i'jaz) menjadi salah satu pembahasan paling canggih dan paling banyak dikutip tentang subyek tersebut dalam literatur Islam klasik.

Doktrin kemukjizatan Al-Quran menyatakan bahwa Al-Quran adalah mukjizat yang membuktikan kenabian Muhammad: ia melampaui kemampuan manusia manapun atau kelompok manusia manapun untuk menghasilkan sesuatu yang sebanding. Al-Quran sendiri menerbitkan tantangan (tahaddi) kepada mereka yang meragukan asal-usul Ilahinya: buatlah satu surat yang sebanding. Bahwa tantangan ini tidak terpenuhi -- meskipun ada budaya sastra yang luar biasa di Arabia abad ketujuh dan motivasi yang kuat dari lawan-lawan Nabi untuk mendiskreditkan pesannya -- diambil sebagai bukti asal-usul Al-Quran yang melampaui manusia.

Kontribusi al-Baqillani adalah menganalisis dengan tepat apa yang membuat Al-Quran tak tertandingi. Diskusi-diskusi sebelumnya kadang-kadang samar pada poin ini. Ia mengidentifikasi beberapa dimensi yang berbeda. Secara linguistik, Al-Quran mencapai tingkat kefasihan Arab dan kesempurnaan stilistik -- dalam pilihan kata, struktur kalimat, ritme, dan koherensi keseluruhan -- yang tidak ditandingi oleh penulis manusia manapun. Ini bukan sekadar masalah preferensi estetis tetapi dapat dianalisis melalui kategori-kategori retorika Arab (balagha), menunjukkan secara spesifik mengapa bahasa Al-Quran lebih unggul.

Melampaui keunggulan linguistik, al-Baqillani menunjuk pada kandungan Al-Quran: kisah-kisahnya tentang peristiwa masa lalu yang tidak dapat diakses oleh Muhammad melalui jalur sejarah normal, prediksi-prediksinya yang akurat tentang peristiwa-peristiwa masa depan, ajaran etis dan hukumnya yang komprehensif dan koheren, serta dampak psikologis dan spiritualnya pada mereka yang menerimanya. Dimensi-dimensi kemukjizatan ini melampaui yang semata-mata sastra.

Al-Baqillani juga berhati-hati dalam mendefinisikan apa yang perbandingan itu libatkan. Ia menganalisis puisi Arab pra-Islam -- mu'allaqat dan teks-teks lain yang dirayakan -- dan menunjukkan secara spesifik bagaimana mereka berbeda dari Al-Quran. Ia tidak meremehkan pencapaian sastra Arab tetapi menggunakannya untuk menyoroti kualitas-kualitas Al-Quran yang khas dan tak tertandingi. Pendekatan analitis ini menjadikan pembahasan i'jaz-nya sebagai model bagi ulama-ulama berikutnya dan referensi standar dalam bidang ilmu-ilmu Al-Quran.

Doktrin i'jaz tetap signifikan dalam wacana Islam kontemporer, khususnya dalam keterlibatan dengan pendekatan-pendekatan sekuler atau kritis terhadap Al-Quran yang memperlakukannya sebagai dokumen sastra manusia. Kerangka analitis al-Baqillani, meskipun dikembangkan dalam konteks klasik, menyediakan perangkat untuk artikulasi kontemporer klaim tradisional ini.
content_ar: Al-Baqillani made a lasting contribution to Islamic scholarship through his separate treatise I'jaz al-القرآن الكريم and through the treatment of the same subject in At-Tamhid. His analysis of القرآني inimitability (i'jaz) became one of the most sophisticated and widely cited treatments of the subject in الكلاسيكي Islamic literature. The doctrine of القرآني inimitability holds that the القرآن الكريم is a miracle that demonstrates محمد ﷺ's prophethood: it is beyond the capacity of any human being or group of human beings to produce anything comparable. The القرآن الكريم itself issues a challenge (tahaddi) to those who doubt its divine origin: produce a comparable الباب. That this challenge was not met — رغم the extraordinary literary culture of سابعاً-century Arabia and the strong motivation of النبي ﷺ's opponents to discredit his message — is taken as evidence of the القرآن الكريم's superhuman origin. Al-Baqillani's contribution was to analyze precisely what makes the القرآن الكريم inimitable. Earlier discussions had sometimes been vague on this point. He identified several distinct dimensions. Linguistically, the القرآن الكريم achieves a level of Arabic eloquence and stylistic perfection — in its word choice, sentence structure, rhythm, and overall coherence — that no human author has matched. This is not merely a matter of aesthetic preference but can be analyzed through the categories of Arabic rhetoric (balagha), showing specifically why the القرآن الكريم's language is superior. Beyond linguistic excellence, al-Baqillani points to the القرآن الكريم's content: its accounts of past events that محمد ﷺ had no access to through normal historical channels, its accurate predictions of future events, its comprehensive and coherent ethical and legal teaching, and its psychological and spiritual impact on those who receive it. These dimensions of inimitability extend beyond the purely literary. Al-Baqillani is أيضاً careful to define what the comparison involves. He analyzes pre-Islamic Arabic poetry — the mu'allaqat and other celebrated texts — and shows specifically how they differ from the القرآن الكريم. He is not dismissive of Arabic literary achievement but uses it to highlight the distinctive and unmatched qualities of the القرآن الكريم. This analytical approach made his treatment of i'jaz a model for subsequent العلماء and a standard reference in the field of القرآني sciences. The doctrine of i'jaz remains المهم in contemporary Islamic discourse, particularly in engagements with secular or البالغ الأهمية approaches to the القرآن الكريم that treat it as a human literary document. Al-Baqillani's analytical framework, بينما developed in a الكلاسيكي context, provides tools for contemporary articulation of the التقليدي claim.
